data.description:
# Chunking, proportionate-depth routing, budget cap

## Why
The gate serves a diverse client base on varied tickets; chunk size, review depth, and budget must adapt to model + ticket size + complexity, with grounded defaults, or the gate is either too costly or too shallow.

## What
The exec-tier-aware chunker policy, proportionate-depth routing, model escalation, and the per-plan budget cap with INDETERMINATE shedding.

## Scope
- Chunk size (single-turn tier only) = base_chunk(model) x size_factor(ticket), config-overridable; defaults grounded by experiment (docs/experiments/). Prompt caching on (system+plan prefix).
- AGENT criteria: one agent each, gated by proportionate scrutiny (run only when warranted by complexity/has_children).
- Escalation to a stronger model by size + complexity + centrality (centrality/blast-radius computed at plan time).
- Per-plan budget cap (wall-clock + cost), chunk-atomic checkpointing; on cap-hit emit INDETERMINATE and shed lowest-priority AGENT/overlay criteria first, never the cheap base.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Chunk size is computed from model + ticket-size signals via the documented formula, config-overridable; unit-tested across model/size combinations.
- [ ] Prompt caching is applied to the system+plan prefix (verified via cache-read usage in a test).
- [ ] A per-plan budget cap halts at chunk boundaries and emits INDETERMINATE, shedding AGENT/overlay criteria before base; tested.
- [ ] Proportionate-depth routing runs the AGENT tier only when complexity/has_children warrant it.
data.title: Chunking, proportionate-depth routing, budget cap
